BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". 1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""STEP 15: compose the REAL scoreable multi-section report from the LIVE agentic run.
3	
4	The mission's metric-(a) full-corpus gate (cp4_used=agentic on the 329-basket corpus) already
5	PASSED (docs/agentic_sweep_live_summary_s3gear329.json). What was missing was a *composed*
6	report we can score. This driver closes that gap: it runs the FULL generator
7	(``generate_multi_section_report``) with the agentic outliner ON (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 + the
8	§9.1.8 model lock) over data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json and writes report.md.
9	
10	Model it on scripts/run_honest_on_prerebuild_corpus.py (which already produced report.md +
11	multi_section_outline.json), minus the retrieval/scope machinery (the corpus is pre-built).
12	
13	Faithfulness gate (HARD): after composition, assert ZERO unverified numbers reach any
14	[CITE:ev_xxx] token in the composed report. The strict_verify lane already enforces this
15	per-section; this driver re-audits the final assembled text as an independent tripwire.
16	
17	Run (key MUST be in env):
18	    set -a && . ./.env && set +a
19	    PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 python scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py \
20	        --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json
21	"""
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import argparse
25	import asyncio
26	import json
27	import logging
28	import os
29	import re
30	import sys
31	import time
32	from pathlib import Path
33	from types import SimpleNamespace
34	
35	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
36	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
37	
38	DRB_QUERY = ROOT / "third_party" / "deep_research_bench" / "data" / "prompt_data" / "query.jsonl"
39	
40	# STEP 2 (wheel: topic-driven structure) — the section headings are now produced TOPIC-DRIVEN by
41	# the generator itself (facet outline + general research-report skeleton: PG_FACET_OUTLINE=1 +
42	# PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON=1). The prior STEP-16 approach hardcoded a clinical-archetype ->
43	# AI/labor relabel MAP here — an overfit band-aid tuned to one benchmark task. That map is GONE:
44	# the outliner emits real topical titles (Introduction / thematic bodies / Cross-Study Synthesis /
45	# Conclusions and Research Gaps) for ANY domain, so assembly renders the section titles verbatim.
46	
47	
48	def _derive_title(rq: str) -> str:
49	    """Derive a neutral report title from the research question — GENERAL, not tuned to any task.
50	
51	    Takes the first sentence/clause of the RQ, strips a leading imperative ("Please write a ...",
52	    "Research ...", "I am researching ..."), and Title-cases nothing (keeps the RQ's own wording).
53	    Falls back to a generic label. No topic is hardcoded."""
54	    import re as _re
55	    s = (rq or "").strip().replace("\n", " ")
56	    s = _re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s)
57	    # First sentence only.
58	    s = _re.split(r"(?<=[.?!])\s", s, maxsplit=1)[0]
59	    # Strip common leading imperatives so the title reads as a subject, not a command.
60	    s = _re.sub(r"^(please\s+)?(help me\s+)?(write|prepare|produce|conduct|research(ing)?|"
61	                r"provide|create|complete|collect( and)?( organi[sz]e)?|i am researching|"
62	                r"i would like|i need)\b[:,]?\s*", "", s, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)
63	    s = s.strip().rstrip(".").strip()
64	    if not s:
65	        return "Research Report"
66	    # Capitalize the first letter only (preserve proper-noun casing in the rest).
67	    return s[0].upper() + s[1:]
68	
69	
70	def _load_drb_prompt(task_id: str) -> str:
71	    """Load a DeepResearch-Bench task's EXACT prompt verbatim (target/ref/criteria all key on it)."""
72	    for line in DRB_QUERY.read_text().splitlines():
73	        o = json.loads(line)
74	        if str(o.get("id")) == str(task_id):
75	            return o["prompt"]
76	    raise SystemExit(f"BLOCKED: DRB task id {task_id} not in {DRB_QUERY}")
77	
78	logging.basicConfig(
79	    level=os.environ.get("PG_LOG_LEVEL", "INFO"),
80	    format="%(asctime)s %(name)s %(levelname)s %(message)s",
81	    datefmt="%H:%M:%S",
82	    stream=sys.stdout,
83	)
84	for noisy in ("httpx", "httpcore"):
85	    logging.getLogger(noisy).setLevel(logging.WARNING)
86	log = logging.getLogger("compose")

89	def _tier_fractions(evidence: list[dict]) -> dict[str, float]:
90	    from collections import Counter
91	    c = Counter((e.get("tier") or "T?").upper() for e in evidence)
92	    n = sum(c.values()) or 1
93	    return {k: v / n for k, v in sorted(c.items())}
94	
95	
96	# A numeric token that would be a faithfulness breach if it sat inside a [CITE:] sentence
97	# without having passed strict_verify. We audit the FINAL assembled report: any [CITE:ev_xxx]
98	# in the verified text is, by construction, already span-grounded — but we re-scan to prove it.
99	_CITE_RE = re.compile(r"\[CITE:(ev_[0-9a-fA-F]+|[a-z0-9_]+)\]")
100	
101	
102	def _audit_citations(report_text: str, biblio: list[dict]) -> dict:
103	    """Independent faithfulness tripwire on the FINAL assembled report.
104	
105	    strict_verify resolves every kept sentence's provenance token into a global [N] bibliography
106	    marker and DROPS any sentence whose number failed the span match. So in a faithful final
107	    report: (1) ZERO raw [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens survive (any survivor is an unverified-number leak
108	    — the exact breach the mission forbids), and (2) every [N] marker in the prose resolves to a
109	    real bibliography entry. We assert both."""
110	    leaked_cites = _CITE_RE.findall(report_text)
111	    body = report_text.split("\n\n## References\n", 1)[0]  # markers in prose only
112	    n_markers = set(int(m) for m in re.findall(r"\[(\d+)\]", body))
113	    biblio_nums = {int(b.get("num")) for b in biblio if str(b.get("num", "")).isdigit()}
114	    unresolved = sorted(n for n in n_markers if n not in biblio_nums)
115	    return {
116	        "leaked_cite_ev_tokens": len(leaked_cites),
117	        "leaked_cite_samples": sorted(set(leaked_cites))[:10],
118	        "distinct_bib_markers_in_prose": len(n_markers),
119	        "bibliography_entries": len(biblio_nums),
120	        "unresolved_markers": unresolved,
121	    }
122	
123	
124	def _order_sections_by_required(verified: list, required_titles: list[str]) -> list:
125	    """Reorder VERIFIED sections so those matching the required titles come first,
126	    in the required order; all others keep their original relative order after.
127	
128	    Matching is case-insensitive containment (a required title matched against a
129	    produced heading). ORDER-ONLY: every input section is present in the output
130	    exactly once — nothing is dropped or fabricated. A required title with no
131	    matching verified section is simply skipped (disclosure is the audit's job)."""
132	    want = [(t or "").strip().lower() for t in required_titles if (t or "").strip()]
133	    used: set[int] = set()
134	    ordered: list = []
135	    for w in want:
136	        for i, sr in enumerate(verified):
137	            if i in used:
138	                continue
139	            tl = (sr.title or "").strip().lower()
140	            if tl == w or w in tl or tl in w:
141	                ordered.append(sr)
142	                used.add(i)
143	                break
144	    for i, sr in enumerate(verified):
145	        if i not in used:
146	            ordered.append(sr)
147	    return ordered
148	
149	
150	def _dedup_biblio_by_work(biblio: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
151	    """Collapse bibliography rows that resolve to the same underlying WORK.
152	
153	    Key is the normalized URL when present, else the normalized statement text.
154	    The FIRST row for each work is kept (preserving its assigned number); later
155	    duplicates are removed. This drops only DUPLICATE reference lines — never a
156	    distinct source — and does not touch in-prose [N] markers."""
157	    seen: set[str] = set()
158	    out: list[dict] = []
159	    for b in biblio:
160	        url = str(b.get("url", "") or "").strip().lower().rstrip("/")
161	        key = url or str(b.get("statement", "") or "").strip().lower()[:160]
162	        if key and key in seen:
163	            continue
164	        if key:
165	            seen.add(key)
166	        out.append(b)
167	    return out
168

170	async def main() -> int:
171	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
172	    ap.add_argument("--corpus", required=True)
173	    ap.add_argument("--out-dir", default=None)
174	    ap.add_argument("--max-parallel", type=int, default=3)
175	    ap.add_argument("--rq-drb-task", default="72",
176	                    help="override the corpus RQ with this DRB task's verbatim prompt so the "
177	                         "composed report answers the SAME task it is scored against; empty string "
178	                         "keeps the corpus RQ")
179	    ap.add_argument("--title", default=None,
180	                    help="report title for the judged report.md; default DERIVES it from the RQ "
181	                         "(general — no title is hardcoded to any task)")
182	    # S4 render projection (default-OFF): when a pinned planning_gate_artifact.json is
183	    # supplied, thread its deliverable/scope/compose projections into composition and
184	    # run the disclosure-only contract-compliance audit alongside the (untouched)
185	    # citation audit. Omitted (the default) => byte-identical to today's champion.
186	    ap.add_argument("--gate-artifact", default=None,
187	                    help="OPTIONAL path to a pinned planning_gate_artifact.json. When given, its "
188	                         "contract projections steer contract-aware assembly (required sections/"
189	                         "order, references dedup) and the compose voice, and audit_contract runs. "
190	                         "Absent => byte-identical champion behavior (no gate).")
191	    args = ap.parse_args()
192	
193	    if not os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"):
194	        log.error("BLOCKED: OPENROUTER_API_KEY not in env — source .env first "
195	                  "(set -a && . ./.env && set +a)")
196	        return 2
197	    # The mission model-lock: agentic outliner ON.
198	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT", "1")
199	    # P0 CONFIRMED-SAFE COMPOSE CONFIG (2026-07-12) — PIN the non-deadlocking config in the launch
200	    # path. The clean 24.2min/1449.7s run used exactly this: off-loop ON (shipped, verdict-safe),
201	    # PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS=1 (serial byte-identical MAP+REDUCE — NEVER >1 without a full-328
202	    # verdict-identity A/B), PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY in the 4-8 band (NEVER >=48), and
203	    # PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=3. These are setdefault (an explicit operator override still wins) but they
204	    # keep this driver on the certified-safe path; the startup guard (compose_config_guard) refuses the
205	    # deadlocking regime regardless. Faithfulness-neutral: pure concurrency knobs.
206	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS", "1")
207	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY", "8")
208	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS", "3")
209	    # P1-SPEED (2026-07-12) — collapse the ISOLATED pre-compose credibility member-verify pass.
210	    # ROOT-CAUSE of the 43min (2589.7s) >> 24min (1449.7s) gap, MEASURED from the phase timeline in
211	    # logs/step3_full328_render.log: threading the PSL gov_suffixes (below) to lift route_all basket
212	    # utilization ALSO activates the ADVISORY credibility corroboration pass. On this 997-member corpus
213	    # that pass ran SERIALLY (PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT default=1) and BANKED at its
214	    # wall*0.85 soft deadline = 1020s, verifying only 207/997 members — a full +1020s phase the 1449.7s
215	    # baseline NEVER ran (it did not thread gov_suffixes -> credibility degraded-to-unscored, skipped).
216	    # This pass runs ENTIRELY BEFORE compose (an ISOLATED flat phase — NO PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS x
217	    # PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS x inner-TPE nesting), so bounding its OWN loop concurrency is NOT the
218	    # multiplicative compose oversubscription the deadlock guard protects against. Parallelize the
219	    # member-verify loop and raise the side-judge cap FOR THIS PHASE ONLY (the designed I-deepfix-001
220	    # box2 lever; credibility_pass_concurrency RESTORES the compose-time cap of 8 before compose starts).
221	    # Faithfulness-neutral & UNDERCOUNT-only: the pass is ADVISORY (strict_verify / 4-role D8 /
222	    # span-grounding are untouched); verifying MORE members in LESS time yields STRICTLY MORE
223	    # corroboration than the 207-serial run and far more than the baseline's zero. All env-overridable.
224	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT", "16")
225	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_SIDE_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY", "16")
226	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_WALL_S", "600")
227	    # STEP 2: topic-driven, synthesis-enabling structure. Facet outline (thematic sections emerge
228	    # from the evidence) + the general research-report skeleton (intro / thematic bodies /
229	    # cross-study synthesis+contradictions / conclusions+gaps). GENERAL structural flags — they
230	    # hardcode no topic and are overridable from the environment.
231	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_FACET_OUTLINE", "1")
232	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON", "1")
233	    # STEP 3 (INSIGHT depth): make the cross-study synthesis section quantify agreement/disagreement
234	    # across the [ev]-backed body figures (enrich its evidence + directive). GENERAL structural
235	    # lever — role detected structurally, no topic/title hardcoded; strict_verify unchanged.
236	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_SYNTHESIS_QUANT_DIRECTIVE", "1")
237	    # STEP 4 (UTILIZATION — the basket under-utilization ghost): the live LLM outline lists only a
238	    # handful of ev_ids per section, so ~90% of the consolidated baskets reach NO section and never
239	    # compose a cited claim (measured 31/329 rendered; scripts/measure_utilization_route_all.py). Route
240	    # every ORPHAN basket to its best-matching thematic section by claim-vs-title content overlap (else a
241	    # single keep-all residual section). GENERAL, faithfulness-neutral: pure CONSOLIDATE placement —
242	    # drops no source, caps nothing; every routed basket's rendered sentence re-passes the UNCHANGED
243	    # strict_verify per clause. Deterministic A/B proved 31->328 baskets rendered. Also drop the
244	    # PG_MAX_EV_PER_SECTION row-cap ceiling so a facet keeps its full matched payload.
245	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS", "1")
246	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_EV_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD", "1")
247	
248	    corpus_path = Path(args.corpus)
249	    corpus = json.loads(corpus_path.read_text())
250	    corpus_rq = corpus["research_question"]
251	    if args.rq_drb_task:
252	        rq = _load_drb_prompt(args.rq_drb_task)
253	        log.info("RQ OVERRIDE: composing to DRB task %s verbatim prompt (corpus RQ kept as "
254	                 "provenance only). task_rq[:90]=%r", args.rq_drb_task, rq[:90])
255	    else:

288	    # baskets. Without gov_suffixes the pre-run guard DEGRADES to credibility_analysis=None (the
289	    # 794->9 collapse), which strands EVERY basket and makes PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS inert — the report
290	    # then renders only the LLM-writer's directly-cited sources. Faithfulness-neutral: priors weights
